gettingStarted: Create your account with email or Google, or use Guest mode.
Set up your profile, photo, and privacy in Settings.
Choose your preferred language (ES/EN) and visual theme.
Explore the dashboard and get familiar with the sidebar menu.
On mobile, use the hamburger button to navigate between sections.
accountsAndCurrencies: Select the currency: USD, EUR, VES, COP, USDT, SOL, BTC, or USDC.
Assign a descriptive name and an initial balance.
Create as many accounts as you need in different currencies.
Use the BCV or P2P rate selector to view bolivar conversions.
finances: Review your favorite accounts on the Dashboard.
Record transactions: Income, Expense, or Transfer.
Use filters and search to find specific movements.
Check the financial performance chart by period.
Use VEPay to automatically capture payment receipts.
financialPlans: Go to Financial Plans and click "New Plan".
Select the type: Savings, Planned Expense, or Recurring.
Set a name, category (custom if you want), and target amount.
Set a due date and, for recurring plans, the frequency.
Share the plan with other users to split expenses.
calendarAndReminders: Go to the Calendar and select a day.
Click "+ Reminder" to create a new one.
Set a title, description, time, and type (custom if needed).
Plans with a due date appear automatically on the calendar.
settings: Go to Settings > Profile for your name, avatar, and currency.
Use the language selector to switch between Spanish and English.
Set your privacy to Public or Private.
Choose your preferred rate: official BCV or Binance P2P.
Customize the visual theme: Light, Dark, or AMOLED.
Customize your dashboard from Settings > Dashboard or the floating 'Customize' button.
dashboard: Click the green 'Customize' button at the bottom-right of the dashboard.
Create categories with names and SVG icons to organize your widgets.
Go to the Catalog and click a widget to add it to your dashboard.
Adjust each widget's size: Small (1 col), Medium (2 cols), or Large (3 cols).
Reorder widgets with arrows and edit titles by clicking on them.
notifications: Turn on the daily balance summary.
Grant permission for browser push notifications.
security: Use a strong, unique password for your account.
Keep your profile private if you don't want to be found easily.
Report suspicious activity from the help chat.
general: The dashboard is at the root (/).

vepay.title: VEPay — Receipt Capture
vepay.subtitle: Smart data extraction for Venezuelan mobile payment receipts

steps.title: Upload your screenshot
steps.description: Take a screenshot from your banking app (BDV, Mercantil, Provincial, Banesco, etc.) and upload it to Prosper Pro.

steps.title: OCR processes automatically
steps.description: The system uses optical character recognition to read the text. It supports more than 20 Venezuelan banks.

steps.title: Data extracted
steps.description: The following are extracted: bank, amount in Bs., reference, date, origin, recipient, and concept.

steps.title: Smart validation
steps.description: The system validates each receipt and shows missing fields. You can review the raw text and make corrections.

items.q: How do I create my Prosper Pro account?
items.a: You can sign up with your email address and a password, or use the quick Google sign-in. Once registration is complete, you'll automatically access the main dashboard.

items.q: Can I use the app without signing in?
items.a: Yes. Guest mode lets you explore the app and view all features without creating an account. When you're ready, you can sign up and keep your data.

items.q: How do I change the app language?
items.a: Go to Settings and use the language selector to switch between Spanish and English. Your preference is saved automatically and applied instantly across the entire interface.

items.q: How do I switch between light and dark mode?
items.a: Click the moon or sun icon in the top bar. You can choose light, dark, or pure black AMOLED mode. Your preference is automatically saved to your profile.

items.q: How do I set up my profile and privacy?
items.a: Go to Settings > Profile. There you can change your name, profile picture, default display currency, and set whether your profile is public or private. A private profile can only be found by exact email.

items.q: How do I navigate the app?
items.a: The sidebar menu gives you access to Dashboard, Financial Plans, Calendar, Finances, Settings, and Help. On mobile, use the hamburger menu button. The top bar includes quick access to notifications, search, and theme switching.

items.q: How do I create accounts in different currencies?
items.a: Go to Finances > New Account. Select the desired currency when creating the account. You can have multiple accounts in different currencies simultaneously, each with its own independent balance.

items.q: What currencies and cryptocurrencies does the app support?
items.a: We support fiat currencies: US Dollar (USD), Venezuelan Bolívar (VES), Euro (EUR), and Colombian Peso (COP). We also support cryptocurrencies: Tether (USDT), Solana (SOL), Bitcoin (BTC), and USD Coin (USDC) with real-time prices from Binance.

items.q: Can I convert balances between currencies?
items.a: Yes. In the Finances section you can view your balances converted to your preferred display currency using the official BCV rate or the market P2P price (Binance), depending on your settings.

items.q: What happens to transactions if I delete an account?
items.a: When you delete an account, all transactions associated with that account are also permanently deleted. We recommend exporting or reviewing your history before deleting an account. This action cannot be undone.

items.q: Can I change the currency of an existing account?
items.a: No, you cannot change the currency of an already created account. If you need an account in another currency, you must create a new one. This prevents confusion in the transaction history and maintains balance integrity.

items.q: What are account groups?
items.a: Groups let you organize your accounts by categories like 'Banks', 'Cryptos', or 'Cash'. Go to Finances, select an account, and use 'Move to group'. You can create, edit, and delete groups from the same panel.

items.q: How does the P2P rate work for cryptocurrencies?
items.a: The P2P rate uses real market prices from Binance. When creating a crypto account (USDT, SOL, BTC, USDC) you can choose whether to use the official BCV rate or P2P. You can switch modes at any time from the Finances toggle or Settings.

items.q: How do I record a transaction?
items.a: Go to Finances and click "New Transaction". Select the type (Income, Expense, or Transfer), account, category, amount, and an optional description. You can also use VEPay to capture receipts automatically.

items.q: How do I show and hide account balances?
items.a: In Finances you'll see each account's balance on cards. You can tap the eye icon next to any balance to hide or show it. This is useful in public or when sharing your screen.

items.q: How do I make quick transfers between accounts?
items.a: Use the "Quick Transfer" feature in Finances. Select the source account, destination account, and amount. The system automatically records an outgoing transaction in one account and an incoming transaction in the other.

items.q: How do I filter and search transactions?
items.a: Use the top filter bar to filter by account, transaction type, and category. You can also use the search field to find transactions by description, amount, or date. History loads 5 at a time for better performance.

items.q: What are favorite accounts?
items.a: You can mark up to 3 accounts as favorites from the Finances section. The "My Accounts" widget on the Dashboard will show only these accounts for a cleaner, more focused view. You can remove them from favorites at any time.

items.q: What does the financial chart show?
items.a: The chart compares your income versus expenses for the selected period (day, week, month, or year). It uses colored bars to visualize trends and see your financial progress at a glance.

items.q: What is VEPay and how do I capture receipts?
items.a: VEPay is Prosper Pro's OCR system for capturing Venezuelan mobile payment receipts. Upload a screenshot from your banking app and the system automatically extracts the bank, amount, reference, date, origin, recipient, and concept.

items.q: How do I create a savings plan?
items.a: Go to Financial Plans > New Plan. Select type "Savings", set a name, category, target amount, and due date. The plan will appear on your dashboard and calendar so you can track your progress.

items.q: What is a planned expense?
items.a: It's a "Expense" type plan to organize future payments. You can contribute gradually and see payment progress in real time. Ideal for organizing large purchases, trips, or debts.

items.q: How do recurring payments work?
items.a: Create a "Recurring" type plan and choose the frequency: daily, weekly, biweekly, monthly, quarterly, or yearly. The system automatically calculates the next due date and lets you record each payment.

items.q: How do I add funds to a plan?
items.a: Click on a savings plan and press "Add Funds". Select the source account and amount. A transaction is automatically created and the plan progress updates instantly.

items.q: Can I share expenses with other users?
items.a: Yes. Within a plan, use the "Share" option and enter the user's name or email. A request is sent that they can accept or reject. Shared plans show who contributed and how much.

items.q: Can I use custom categories?
items.a: Yes. When creating a plan you can choose from predefined categories or enter a new custom category. This lets you organize your plans exactly how you need.

items.q: What statuses can plans have?
items.a: Plans can be: Pending (no funds yet), In Progress (with at least one contribution), Completed (goal reached), or Canceled (archived without completion). You can filter by status in the plans list.

items.q: How do I create a reminder?
items.a: Go to the Calendar, select a day, and click "+ Reminder". Enter a title, description, time, and type. You can also set up recurring reminders so they repeat automatically.

items.q: How do I see my goals and plans on the calendar?
items.a: Plans with due dates and reminders appear automatically on the calendar with their original colors. You can see at a glance which days have scheduled financial activity.

items.q: Can I create custom reminder types?
items.a: Yes. When creating a reminder, type a new name in the type selector if you can't find the one you need. Your custom type will be available for future reminders.

items.q: How do recurring reminders work?
items.a: When creating a reminder, enable the recurrence option and select the frequency (daily, weekly, monthly, etc.). The system will automatically generate future instances on the calendar.

items.q: Can I edit or delete a reminder after creating it?
items.a: Yes. Go to the Calendar, select the day where the reminder is, and click on it. A panel will open where you can edit any field or delete it permanently. Recurring reminders are edited as a single entity.

items.q: Do plans with due dates automatically appear on the calendar?
items.a: Yes. Any financial plan with a due date is automatically displayed on the calendar with a visual indicator. Scheduled recurring payments also appear, so you have all your financial activity in one view.

items.q: How do I customize my dashboard?
items.a: Click the green 'Customize' button at the bottom-right of the dashboard. From there you can create categories, add widgets from the catalog, change sizes (Small/Medium/Large), reorder and remove widgets. You can also access it from Settings > Dashboard.

items.q: What widgets can I add to my dashboard?
items.a: We offer 16 widgets: Welcome Banner, Stats, For Today, Quick Actions, Import Invoice, Shopping Lists, AI Assistant, Monthly Summary, My Accounts, Recent Transactions, Quick Transfer, Active Plans, Upcoming Deadlines, Exchange Rates, Financial Performance, and Currency Converter.

items.q: Can I create my own dashboard categories?
items.a: Yes. In the 'Categories' tab of the customizer you can create new categories with a custom name and SVG icon. You can also edit, reorder, and delete existing categories.

items.q: What can I configure in my profile?
items.a: In Settings > Profile you can change your full name, avatar picture, default display currency, interface language (Spanish/English), and visual theme (light, dark, or AMOLED).

items.q: How do I change the language between Spanish and English?
items.a: In Settings you'll find the language selector. When changed, the entire interface translates instantly, including menus, buttons, notifications, and help content. Your preference is saved to your profile.

items.q: How do I configure my profile privacy?
items.a: In Settings > Profile use the Public/Private toggle. If your profile is private, other users can only find you if they know your exact email. This protects your identity when sharing plans.

items.q: What's the difference between BCV and P2P rates?
items.a: The BCV rate is the official reference from the Central Bank of Venezuela. The P2P rate is the real market price according to Binance. You can choose which one to use to convert your bolivar balances in Settings > Preferences.

items.q: How do I delete all my data?
items.a: In Settings > Danger Zone you'll find the option to permanently delete your account and all your data from Firestore and Storage. You can also use "Clear all" in Accounting Management to reset transactions without deleting your account.

items.q: What are BCV rate change alerts?
items.a: You receive a notification when the official BCV rate experiences a significant change. This helps you stay on top of important changes affecting the value of your bolivar balances.

items.q: What is the daily balance summary?
items.a: It's an automatic notification you receive at the end of the day with a summary of your total balances, daily movements, and plan status. You can turn it on or off in Settings > Notifications.

items.q: How do I enable browser notifications?
items.a: In Settings > Notifications you'll find the toggle to enable browser push notifications. The system will ask for permission and then can send you alerts even when the app is closed.

items.q: What notifications do I get when someone invites me to a plan?
items.a: You'll receive a push notification when someone invites you to collaborate on a financial plan. From the notification you can accept or reject directly. If you reject it, the plan disappears from your list.

items.q: Can I turn off only some notifications?
items.a: Yes. In Settings > Notifications you can individually enable or disable: BCV rate change alerts, daily balance summary, plan invitations, plan contributions, and browser push notifications.

items.q: Do notifications work on mobile?
items.a: Yes, push notifications work on mobile devices if you use Chrome, Safari, or Edge and have granted permission. If you don't see notifications, check that your browser has permissions enabled in the system settings.

items.q: Is my data secure?
items.a: Yes. We use Firebase with strict security rules that isolate data by ownerId. Only you can access your information. Passwords are handled through Firebase Auth with industry-standard encryption.

items.q: Can I use Prosper Pro on multiple devices?
items.a: Yes. Your data syncs in real time through Firestore. You can access it from your phone, tablet, or computer and see the same updated information instantly across all devices.

items.q: How do I permanently delete my account?
items.a: Go to Settings > Danger Zone and click "Delete my account forever". All your data from Firestore, Storage, and Authentication will be deleted. This action is irreversible.

items.q: What if I forget my password?
items.a: On the sign-in screen, click "Forgot your password?". Enter your email and you'll receive a secure link to reset it. If you used Google Sign-In, recover your account directly from Google.

items.q: Who can see my financial data?
items.a: No one but you. Your transactions, accounts, and plans are isolated by ownerId in Firebase. Not even Prosper Pro administrators can access your personal or financial information.

items.q: Is it safe to use my account on public networks?
items.a: Yes. All communication uses HTTPS and TLS. However, as a precaution we recommend not using public Wi-Fi networks for sensitive transactions, and always logging out on shared devices.

items.q: Is Prosper Pro free?
items.a: Yes. All current and future Prosper Pro features are available at no cost. There are no paid plans or hidden usage limits.

items.q: What URL is the dashboard on?
items.a: The main dashboard is at the root (/). When you sign in, the home page automatically becomes your personalized control panel.

items.q: Does Prosper Pro work without an internet connection?
items.a: No. Prosper Pro is a web application that requires an internet connection to sync your data with Firebase. However, once loaded, some views may remain visible thanks to the browser cache.

items.q: Can I export my data?
items.a: There is currently no automatic export function, but you can copy your balances and transactions manually. We are working on an Excel/CSV export feature for future versions.

items.q: How do I report a bug or suggest an improvement?
items.a: In the Help section you'll find the Feedback & Bugs chat. You can send bug reports or suggestions directly. Our team reviews them regularly and incorporates them into updates.

items.q: How often is the app updated?
items.a: We release updates every few days with improvements, fixes, and new features. You can see the full history in Help > Release Notes. The what's new modal will automatically notify you of each new version.

chat.title: Feedback & Bugs
chat.subtitle: Report bugs or send suggestions
chat.thanks: Thanks for your feedback. We'll review it soon.
chat.hint: Be detailed so we can help you better.
chat.error: Failed to send. Please try again.

items: Planned expenses: total amount is now auto-calculated from sub-plans.
Modals are now vertically centered in the visible screen area.
Minor bug fixes and stability improvements.
New sub-plans for planned expenses: split a large expense into partial payments with different currencies.
Recurring calculation now uses the actual days of the current month (average per month).
Fixed multi-currency calculation in plans and recurring expenses.
Prosper Pro is now web-only: removed the Android/Capacitor layer.
Removed device admin system: any device can close any session.
Fixed login/logout flicker bug that kicked users out right after signing in.
Fixed duplicate user dropdown in the top-right corner.
Recurring plans now auto-reset when the cycle expires (current = 0, nextDueDate advances).
Fixed update modal icons showing text instead of emojis.
Fully customizable dashboard: create categories, add widgets, resize them, and pick SVG icons.
New login notifications across all your connected devices.
Device management: heartbeat, online/offline status, and remote session logout.
Fixed BCV rate change notifications that were not firing correctly.
Landing page updated with the customizable dashboard feature.
Help center expanded with dashboard customization guides.
Full English & Spanish internationalization (i18n) support.
Guest Mode to explore the app without signing in.
Full English & Spanish internationalization (i18n) support.
Guest Mode to explore the app without signing in.
Daily frequency added to recurring financial plans.
Real-time BCV rate change notifications.
Landing page redesigned: faster, more accessible, and with smoother animations.
Help center overhaul: 9 categories, 56 FAQs, and step-by-step tutorials.
App notifications subscription from the landing page footer.
Persistent language switcher that saves your preference across the site.
Favorite accounts: mark up to 3 accounts and the Dashboard will show only those for a cleaner view.
Profile menu fixed: Settings, theme switch and Log Out respond correctly on desktop and mobile.
Update modal restored: now displays automatically whenever there's a new version.
Performance optimizations: better Lighthouse score, faster loading, and less resource usage.
Dashboard duplicate fix: accounts no longer appear duplicated when syncing from Firestore.
Removed flicker effect when expanding Dashboard sections: smoother, more stable transitions.
Landing page completely redesigned to be more interactive, dynamic, and free of empty spaces.
New Interactive Demo on the landing page: explore mockups of Dashboard, Finances, Goals, Calendar, and Academy.
Testimonials now in automatic carousel with manual navigation and more user stories.
New Security section with interactive cards explaining how we protect your data.
"How It Works" steps are now clickable, with progress bar and details per step.
All feature cards now include animated visuals and hover effects.
Animated counters in the trust bar that activate on scroll.
Newsletter form in the footer is now functional with validation and confirmation messages.
Favorite accounts: you can now mark up to 3 accounts as favorites from the Finances section.
The "My Accounts" Dashboard widget was refreshed to show only your favorite accounts.
If you have no favorites, the Dashboard will tell you how to add them to customize your main view.
You can remove an account from favorites directly from the Dashboard or Finances with one click.
The limit of 3 favorites is maintained to keep the Dashboard clean and focused on what matters most.
Performance optimization: code split with dynamic imports to reduce initial bundle.
Next.js optimized images (automatic WebP/AVIF formats).
New P2P cryptocurrencies: Bitcoin (BTC) and USD Coin (USDC) with real Binance prices.
Landing page completely refreshed with testimonials, interactive FAQ, trust bar, and tutorials.
Smart redirect: /dashboard redirects to / to avoid landing page duplication.
Optimized CSS animations: lower CPU usage on low-end devices.
P2P rates: now you can choose between the official BCV rate or the real market price (Binance) when viewing your bolivar balances.
Improved notifications: delete notifications one by one or clear them all at once.
Finances movement history loads 5 at a time for better speed.
Mobile-adapted design: buttons, tables, and menus look better on small screens.
Simplified dashboard: removed the Savings section to focus on income, expenses, and balance.
Dashboard stats now show 3 indicators in a single row.
Dashboard works correctly on mobile: CSS moved to its own file (dashboard.css), conflicts resolved with globals.css.
Overall Progress widget fully functional: now counts goals and plans, including shared plans.
Animations with prefers-reduced-motion fallback to avoid invisible elements on devices with reduced motion preference.
Fixed stats-grid forcing 4 columns at 1280px (now collapses to 2).
Fixed progress ring stroke-dashoffset showing a pre-filled bar.
Fixed modal-content not respecting max-width: 440px at 480px.
Removed * { box-sizing: border-box } global from dashboard CSS that was overriding system styles.
Dashboard refreshed with 3 new widgets: Month Summary (income/expenses/balance), Latest Movements, and Quick Actions.
Smart arrows in scrollable sections: appear on hover and auto-scroll while the mouse is held.
Improved aesthetics: glassmorphism, premium shadows, smooth transitions, and hover effects on cards.
Collaborative shared plans: plans you're invited to automatically appear in Goals with "Shared" and "Guest" badges.
Per-user contributions: when adding funds to a shared plan, who contributed is recorded and shown on the card.
Global Save Changes bar in Settings, visible from any tab.
Persistent profile privacy: the public/private toggle now saves correctly when clicking Save Changes.
Automatic exclusion of the current user when searching people to share plans.
Firestore rules updated to allow invited users to update shared plans.
Profile privacy: new option in Settings to choose whether your profile is public or private. If private, only found by exact email.
Search by name when sharing plans: now you can type a person's name and a list of results will appear.
Hamburger menu refreshed: now covers the full screen and slides smoothly from the left.
Mobile user menu: tapping your avatar opens a bottom-sheet with rounded corners.
Name visible in the top mobile bar next to the avatar.
Fixed animations: all menus and windows now have consistent, smooth transitions.
What's new window adapted to any screen, anchored at the bottom on mobile.
"In Development" labels added to Languages, Active Sessions, and Email contact.
Update to BETA 0.8.0 with performance improvements.
New financial analysis tools.
Update to BETA 0.8.2 with performance improvements and new features.
New financial analysis and advanced reporting tools.
